critique:
Critique:
Step 1 is incorrect. The use of f_select_row(*) is a misuse of the function: the wildcard (*) is not defined in the provided function descriptions, and selecting all rows does not perform any meaningful filtering or transformation aligned with the question. The question is about the cycle length indicated by the year columns (Year 1, Year 2, Year 3), which can be determined without row selection; if any transformation were needed, focusing on the year columns (e.g., via f_select_column) would be more appropriate.

judge_thought:
Explanation:
The table explicitly organizes the readings into a three-year cycle:
- Year 1 includes 2013, 2016, 2019, 2022, corresponding to verses 6:2–7:7.
- Year 2 includes 2014, 2017, 2020, 2023, corresponding to verses 7:8–8:15.
- Year 3 includes 2015, 2018, 2021, 2024, corresponding to verses 8:16–9:35.
The repetition of the same “year” set of verses occurs every three years (e.g., 2013 repeats in 2016, 2019, 2022), confirming that it takes 3 years to read the same verses again.
